title: Wound closure apparatus and method
abstract: A wound closure apparatus can be a self-contained device for delivery and deployment of a wound plug. The apparatus can include a post having a first end and a second end, wherein the first end of the post includes a coupling attachment. The apparatus can include a rod having a first end, a second end and a rod cavity that extends from the first end of the rod to the second end of the rod. The apparatus can also include a wound plug, having a first end, a second end, and a breakaway connection located between the first end and second end of the wound plug. In an assembled configuration, the post can extend through the rod cavity and be detachably coupled to the breakaway connection such that the first end of the wound plug is positioned adjacent the second end of the rod.
claims: Claims 1. An apparatus for deploying a wound plug, the apparatus comprising: a post having a first end and a second end, wherein the first end of the post includes a coupling attachment; a rod having a first end, a second end, and a rod cavity, the rod cavity extending from the first end of the rod to the second end of the rod; and a wound plug having a first end, a second end, and a breakaway connection, the breakaway connection located between the first end of the wound plug and the second end of the wound plug, wherein the wound plug further includes: a distal portion having a plurality of annular flanges located between the second end of the wound plug and the breakaway connection; and a proximate portion having a plurality of annular grooves located between the first end of the wound plug and the breakaway connection, wherein in an assembled configuration, the post extends through the rod cavity and is capable of being coupled and decoupled to the breakaway connection such that the first end of the wound plug is positioned adjacent to the second end of the rod, and wherein in a deployed configuration, the plurality of annular flanges and the plurality of annular grooves interlock. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein a coupling mechanism of the coupling attachment, and the breakaway connection is one selected from one or more of: a press-fit, a luer lock, a latch, and a cam-lock. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the coupling attachment of the post is a male component, and the breakaway connection of the wound plug is a female component. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the post and the rod can be coupled to a second wound plug. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the second end of the post includes a handle, and the second end of the rod includes an annular plate. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the proximate portion includes a cavity that extends from the first end of the wound plug to the breakaway connection such that the post extends through the proximate portion of the wound plug in the assembled configuration. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein an outer surface of the proximate portion of the wound plug includes a plurality of barbs. 8. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the plurality of barbs is unidirectional, and the plurality of barbs includes pointed features pointing towards the second end of the post. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the plurality of barbs is bidirectional, and the plurality of barbs includes: first barbs having first pointed features pointing towards the second end of the post, and second barbs having second pointed features pointing towards the first end of the wound plug. 10. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first end of the wound plug includes a suprafascial extension, wherein the suprafascial extension is a shape of one or more of: a cup, a stay, and a plate. 11. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the wound plug further comprises at least one stay coupled to the second end of the wound plug. 12. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the wound plug excludes a compressible column. 13. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the apparatus excludes an alignment pin. 14. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the apparatus excludes a shield. 15. A method for deploying a wound plug, the method comprising: inserting at least a portion of an assembled wound plug apparatus into a skin opening, wherein the assembled wound plug apparatus includes a post disposed through a cavity of a rod, wherein a coupling attachment of the post is coupled to the wound plug at a breakaway connection; moving the assembled wound plug apparatus such that a distal portion of the wound plug passes through the skin opening and to a wound; and implanting the wound plug by raising a handle of the post away from the skin opening and pushing a plate of the rod toward the skin opening such that: a plurality of annular flanges disposed on a distal end of the wound plug is pulled in an upward direction by the post, a plurality of annular grooves disposed on a proximate end of the wound plug is pushed downward by the rod, and the plurality of annular flanges engages with the plurality of annular grooves. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising: decoupling the coupling attachment of the post from the breakaway connection of the wound plug, and removing the post from the wound. 17. The method of claim 16, further comprising: reassembling the wound plug apparatus by sliding the post through the cavity of the rod, and coupling the coupling attachment of the post to a breakaway connection of a second wound plug.
